title: Job Lifecycle, Isolation, and Retention
objective: Run each future analysis through a bounded, session-owned, cancellable, crash-recoverable, and time-limited application workspace without exposing payloads, allocating resources after rejection, or claiming production isolation before deployment evidence.
scope: Versioned queue, staging, retention, and mandatory worker-limit policy
Separate cryptographic session capability and job identifier with non-enumerating ownership checks
Payload-free transactional SQLite control store with optimistic state transitions and expiry
Orthogonal execution outcome, cancellation, artifact lifecycle, and deletion evidence
Session-owned staged input API with bounded absolute expiry, not yet connected to the public P004 flow
Server-generated input, work, result, export, and control workspaces with confinement and verified cleanup
One-running and three-queued atomic FIFO admission with zero-allocation rejection
Generic fixed-argv process controller with finite resource profile, timeout, cancellation, process-group termination, and reap
Startup recovery and continuous deadline-driven janitor for staged, queued, terminal, export, and log artifacts
Content-free operational events, deletion ledger, canary scans, accessible lifecycle projection, and exact-commit evidence
non_goals: R stylo execution, computational parity, preprocessing, or any scientific result
Public Start analysis activation or P004 Upload-Describe-Review payload connection before P006 and P008
Production CPU, RAM, PID, disk, or timeout numbers before P014 load evidence
Container, cgroup, seccomp, AppArmor, no-new-privileges, egress, proxy, TLS, host, swap, snapshot, backup, or reboot guarantees
Delta-LDA production isolation, Lemmata load testing, deployment, or rollback
Secure or forensic erasure, browser-download deletion, or complete CE-14/CE-15 verification
Distributed queue, multiple workers, user accounts, login, analytics, runtime AI, or permanent project history

acceptance.criterion: A versioned policy and deterministic state model separate execution outcome, cancellation, artifact lifecycle, immutable deadlines, and deletion evidence; every illegal or conflicting transition fails closed while repeated operations are idempotent.

Session capability and job ID are independently generated with at least 256 bits of entropy; every status, cancel, result, export, and cleanup operation enforces server-side ownership, and unauthorized versus unknown requests expose no existence oracle.

Staging and queue admission enforce one staged or active job per session, at most four staged leases, one running job, and three queued jobs transactionally; any rejected submission allocates no identifier, workspace, process, queue slot, or event.

Job workspaces use only trusted roots and server identifiers, apply private modes, reject symlink, hardlink, path, and rename-swap attacks, and never alter an external canary during materialization or cleanup.

A generic fixture worker cannot start without finite wall, CPU, RAM, and PID limits; timeout and idempotent cancellation terminate and reap the complete tested process tree, and completion races produce one terminal outcome without publishing partial output.

Success publishes export only after verified raw and normalized cleanup; failure, cancellation, timeout, crash, abandonment, staged expiry, queue expiry, result/export expiry, event expiry, and restart orphan recovery meet their versioned deadlines under a fake clock and a continuously running janitor.

SQLite, WAL, event logs, deletion ledger, errors, DOM, session state, other sessions, results, and exports contain no canary corpus, filename, scholarly metadata, absolute path, stdout/stderr, or traceback; user-facing lifecycle projections remain accessible and do not activate or imitate scientific analysis.

Concurrency, race, crash, OOM, process-tree, retention, canary, browser-boundary, clean-clone, Linux CI, SBOM, and canonical container gates pass with full measured source coverage, while P014-only production limits remain explicitly unverified.

changed_files: P005 execution brief, independent four-lens architecture audit, proposed lifecycle ADR, active Ticket, and synchronized startup pointers

Lifecycle identity, policy, immutable state model, payload-free SQLite store, private workspace, validated-payload staging, synthetic process controller, deletion-event model, and conservative UI projection

Persistent content-free deletion ledger, fail-closed optional workspace recovery, continuous fake-clock janitor, exact retention deadlines, success publication ordering, and verified-absence tombstone purge
Per-job app-loss guardian, unreaped leader ownership, durable SQLite terminal acknowledgement, execution-bound signed recovery receipt, and real running/completed worker SIGKILL races

Acceptance hardening for capability-first service operations, zero-allocation admission, cleanup-failure accounting, cancellation delivery, reciprocal guardian acknowledgement, descriptor-bound process startup, exact browser evidence, and retained CI supply-chain output
Linux emergency-reap correction, deterministic cross-platform branch coverage, final exact-commit replay, transparent failed-CI sequence, and external artifact-quota boundary
